description: The <0>Model Context Protocol</0> enables communication with locally running MCP servers that provide additional tools and resources to extend CoStrict's capabilities. You can use <1>community-made servers</1> or ask CoStrict to create new tools specific to your workflow (e.g., "add a tool that gets the latest npm docs").<DocsLink>Learn more</DocsLink>

enableToggle.description: When enabled, CoStrict will be able to interact with MCP servers for advanced functionality. If you're not using MCP, you can disable this to reduce CoStrict's token usage.

enableServerCreation.description: When enabled, CoStrict can help you create new MCP servers via commands like "add a new tool to...". If you don't need to create MCP servers, you can disable it to reduce CoStrict's token usage.<DocsLink>Learn more</DocsLink>
enableServerCreation.hint: Hint: To reduce API token costs, disable this setting when you are not actively asking CoStrict to create a new MCP server.
